info.title: Primordia API
info.description: Internal REST API for Primordia — the self-modifying AI web app. This is not a stable public API; it documents internal endpoints as they currently exist and may change at any time. Covers chat, authentication, and the evolve pipeline.

previewInactivityMin.description: * Disk usage % at which automatic worktree cleanup is triggered. Default: 90.

post.summary: Roll back to a previous production slot
post.description: Starts the target worktree's server, health-checks it, then zero-downtime swaps it into production. Admin only.

worktreePath.description: Absolute path of the target registered git worktree to roll back to.

patch.description: Updates one or more reverse-proxy tuning knobs stored in git config. Admin only.

previewInactivityMin.description: Minutes of inactivity before a preview server is stopped.

diskCleanupThresholdPct.description: Disk usage percentage at which automatic worktree cleanup is triggered.

post.summary: Add or update the push mirror remote
post.description: Adds (or updates) a 'mirror' git remote as a push mirror and verifies the connection with an initial push. Admin only.

url.description: SSH or HTTPS URL of the remote repository to mirror pushes to.

post.summary: Grant or revoke a user role
post.description: Grants or revokes a grantable role (e.g. `can_evolve`) for a user. The `admin` role cannot be granted via this endpoint. Admin only.

userId.description: UUID of the user to modify.

role.description: The role to grant or revoke.

action.description: Whether to grant or revoke the role.

post.description: Called by the already-authenticated approver device (e.g. phone) to approve a pending cross-device sign-in token. Requires an active session.

tokenId.description: The cross-device token ID returned by POST /auth/cross-device/start.

post.description: Start a new AI-powered code-change session. Accepts `multipart/form-data` (supports file attachments) or JSON. Requires `can_evolve` or `admin` role.

request.description: The natural-language change request for Claude Code to implement.
request.example: Add a dark mode toggle to the header

harness.description: Agent harness to use (e.g. 'claude-code'). Defaults to the server default.

model.description: AI model identifier to pass to the agent harness.

cavemanMode.description: Enable caveman communication mode for Claude. Pass the string 'true' to enable.

cavemanIntensity.description: Caveman mode intensity level (lite, full, ultra, wenyan-lite, wenyan-full, wenyan-ultra). Only relevant when cavemanMode is 'true'.

encryptedApiKey.description: Optional RSA-OAEP encrypted Anthropic API key (ciphertext from /api/llm-key/public-key). Used instead of the server's gateway key when provided.

attachments.description: Optional file attachments (images, text files, etc.) to include in the session context. Files are copied into the worktree's attachments/ directory.

request.description: The natural-language change request for Claude Code to implement.
request.example: Add a dark mode toggle to the header

encryptedApiKey.description: Optional RSA-OAEP encrypted Anthropic API key.

post.summary: Accept or reject an evolve session
post.description: Accept (deploy) or reject (discard) a ready evolve session. Requires `can_evolve` or `admin` role.

action.description: 'accept' deploys the session branch to production (or merges it in dev). 'reject' discards the worktree and branch.

sessionId.description: The session ID (same as the git branch name) to accept or reject.

post.summary: Merge parent branch into a session
post.description: Merges the session's parent branch into the session worktree to pick up upstream changes. Auto-resolves conflicts via Claude if needed.

sessionId.description: The session ID (git branch name) to sync upstream changes into.

action.description: The sync strategy. Currently only 'merge' is supported.

post.summary: Abort the running Claude Code agent
post.description: Signals the active Claude Code process to stop and transitions the session back to 'ready' with whatever work was completed.

sessionId.description: The session ID (git branch name) of the running session to abort.

post.description: Send an additional change request to an already-ready evolve session. Accepts `multipart/form-data` (supports file attachments) or JSON.

sessionId.description: The session ID (git branch name) of the ready session to continue.

request.description: The follow-up change request text for Claude Code.
request.example: Also update the README to document the new toggle

harness.description: Agent harness override for this follow-up run.

model.description: AI model override for this follow-up run.

encryptedApiKey.description: Optional RSA-OAEP encrypted Anthropic API key.

attachments.description: Optional additional file attachments to include in this follow-up run.

sessionId.description: The session ID (git branch name) of the ready session to continue.

request.description: The follow-up change request text for Claude Code.
request.example: Also update the README to document the new toggle

encryptedApiKey.description: Optional RSA-OAEP encrypted Anthropic API key.

post.summary: Start an evolve session on an existing branch
post.description: Attaches the full AI preview pipeline to an existing local git branch (e.g. from an external contributor). The branch must exist locally and must not contain a slash. Requires `can_evolve` or `admin` role.

branchName.description: Name of the existing local git branch to attach a session to. Must not contain a slash.

post.summary: Kill and restart a session's preview dev server
post.description: Delegates to the reverse proxy to kill the session's running preview dev server process and start a fresh one in the same worktree.

sessionId.description: The session ID (git branch name) whose preview server should be restarted.

post.description: Stores an AES-GCM encrypted Anthropic API key ciphertext for the authenticated user. The server never sees the AES decryption key — only the ciphertext is persisted.

ciphertext.description: Base64-encoded AES-GCM ciphertext of the Anthropic API key.

get.description: Returns a list of missing required server-side environment variables. An empty `missing` array means the server is fully configured.
